storage_extensions.usage: mmap codes.bin; at build-IS-hash-table time, for each IS pre row i: plabel_code = plabel_codes[i] (uint32_t). Store plabel_code in the IS-pre hash table value alongside pre_row_id. Use plabel_code as integer group-by key component, eliminating secondary string grouping. At output decode time: dict_offsets[plabel_code]..dict_offsets[plabel_code+1] gives byte span in dict_data.

join_order.notes: Load sub/fy.bin; build flat bool array fy2023[86135] indexed by adsh_code (84KB, L1-resident). Also load sub/name.bin as flat int32 array for name_code lookup.

join_order.build: pre(stmt=IS) → flat open-addressing hash table keyed by packed(adsh,tag,ver)
join_order.probe: num rows passing fy2023 filter (~6.5M rows)
join_order.notes: Scan pre columns (stmt, adsh, tag, version, plabel_codes) using pre_zonemaps to identify IS-stmt blocks. For each IS row: key = pack_key(adsh_code:17b, tag_code:18b, ver_code:17b) into uint64_t; value = pack_val(pre_row_id:24b, plabel_code:20b) into uint64_t. Hash table: 2^21=2097152 slots, each slot = key(uint64_t) + value(uint64_t) = 16 bytes; total 32MB → fits in L3 (44MB). Replaces O(log 9.6M) binary search into 153MB pre_key_sorted with O(1) L3-resident hash probe.

filters.notes: Load usd_code from shared/uom.dict at runtime; use num_zonemaps to identify USD-containing blocks
Direct bool array lookup fy2023[adsh_code]; ~16219 qualifying adsh codes
Baked into IS-pre hash table at build time; IS rows only are inserted. No per-row check needed during probe.

data_structures.sub_fy_filter: flat_bool_array[86135] — direct index by adsh_code; 84KB; L1-resident
data_structures.sub_name_lookup: flat_int32_array[86135] — direct index by adsh_code; loaded from sub/name.bin
data_structures.is_pre_hashtable: flat open-addressing hash table; 2^21 slots × 16 bytes = 32MB; key=uint64_t packed(adsh:17b|tag:18b|ver:17b); value=uint64_t packed(pre_row_id:24b|plabel_code:20b); built at runtime from pre IS rows; L3-resident
data_structures.plabel_codes: mmap column_versions/pre.plabel.dict/codes.bin; uint32_t[9600799]; read sequentially during IS-pre hash table build
data_structures.thread_local_agg: per-thread flat open-addressing hash map; 2^18=262144 slots × 24 bytes = 6.3MB per thread; key=uint64_t packed(name_code:14b|tag_code:18b|plabel_code:20b); value=struct{double sum; int64_t cnt}=16 bytes; each map is L3-resident per thread
data_structures.global_merge_map: flat open-addressing hash map; 2^17=131072 slots × 24 bytes = 3MB; shared for serial merge phase; L3-resident throughout

parallelism.notes: IS-pre hash table built single-threaded (sequential pre scan, ~30ms). Num scan parallelized across 64 threads using USD block morsels from num_zonemaps. Each thread owns a thread-local flat hash map (~6MB, L3-resident) for aggregation with zero contention. After scan, serial merge of thread-local maps into 3MB global map (all L3-resident; estimated ~30ms vs 466ms for std::unordered_map merge).

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks — identify USD-containing blocks in num; distribute block morsels to threads
skip_blocks — during IS-pre hash table build, use pre_zonemaps to skip non-IS stmt blocks (IS rows are contiguous segment since pre is sorted by (stmt, adsh)); scan only IS-stmt blocks

group_key.notes: plabel_code from pre.plabel.dict eliminates secondary string grouping entirely. All group key components are integer codes — single-pass aggregation with no post-processing re-grouping. Decode at output time only for top 200 rows.

decode_at_output: stmt is always IS (constant string, no dict lookup needed)

optimization_notes: Three root causes identified. (1) decode_and_output (357ms): secondary string re-grouping was architecturally forced because pre_row_id is not a unique plabel identity — different adsh rows share the same plabel string. Fix: Category E, built pre.plabel.dict (dict-encoded uint32 codes for all 9.6M pre rows, 698K unique values). plabel_code is now part of the primary integer group key, eliminating the secondary string grouping pass entirely. (2) merge_aggregate (466ms): 64 std::unordered_map instances collectively exceed L3 (44MB); serial merge is cache-thrashing pointer-chasing. Fix: Category B, replace with flat open-addressing hash maps (2^18 slots × 24 bytes = 6.3MB per thread, L3-resident during scan) and a 3MB global merge target that stays cache-warm throughout serial merge. (3) main_scan (245ms): binary search into 153MB pre_key_sorted produces O(log 9.6M)=23 cache-miss comparisons per qualifying row. Fix: Category A/B, build an IS-only pre hash table at startup (1.73M IS rows → 2M-slot flat table = 32MB, fits in L3) from pre columns using pre_zonemaps to skip non-IS blocks. Hash probe replaces binary search with O(1) L3-resident lookup. Correctness: single-pass aggregation is now complete — no secondary grouping needed because plabel_code directly identifies the plabel string value.
